what_it_does: A lean, second loop-prep implementation guide for the first vertical slice of a new Instrument Surface front-end app (surface/app), specifying architecture, file tree, API contracts, address spine, motion system, three form-factor layouts, build sequence, guardrails, and phase-2 backlog.
observations: Scopes work to one slice: an instrument wheel lens running on port 5174 while leaving the existing canvas/app PWA on 5173 untouched.
Requires every interactive node to carry both a literal data-ui-ref address and a Framer Motion layoutId.
Defines a TypeScript-shaped projection contract and render math derived from /api/projection and /api/context.
Prescribes three independent layout modules selected by viewport: Desktop, Portrait, and Landscape.
States a 'registry-is-truth; zero hardcodes' rule and a 'fail loud, never silent' principle.
Includes a 10-step build sequence ordered to match completion-criteria priority.
Explicitly forbids editing canvas/app or the projection engine for this slice.
standouts: The coupled identity rule: address + layoutId on the same node.
The projection contract and render math are treated as the single source of truth, not inferred.
The resting-state-first design inversion and the paper aesthetic token system.
The three form-factor-specific layout modules sharing one Wheel, Disclosure, address spine, and motion system.
conventions: Uses 'IS' for existing repo state and 'WILL' for planned work.
Mirrors the backend capture-phase data-ui-ref listener convention.
Prefers additive event handling without preventDefault or stopPropagation.
Applies transform+opacity-only animation and honors prefers-reduced-motion.
Uses dyadic seed constants and angle-hue mapping for color.
Avoids hardcoding lens, sector, ring, or color data; derives all from the API.
concerns: The ~3,038-point cloud SVG rendering claim is mentioned but not validated for performance in the slice.
The Taste.tsx toggle is described as a 'slice-only scaffold' and may be under-specified.
The 'three taste toggles' are referenced but not enumerated in detail.
The bottom-sheet and thumb-arc interactions in Portrait are described at a high level without gesture specifics.
The allowedHosts pattern '.tail777bc2.ts.net' is hardcoded in the vite.config.ts file tree.
notes: This is the build plan for the first Instrument Surface slice: a fresh React/Vite app on :5174 that renders a real-data instrument wheel with addressable, animated, form-factor-aware UI.
questions: What exact 'three taste toggles' are required and how do they map to token changes?
What is the precise content and structure of the R2 context bundle returned by /api/context?
How is the 'thumb control arc' in Portrait implemented and what actions does it expose?
What are the exact acceptance criteria in COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md that each build step maps to?
What does the existing canvas/app look like so the 'never look at old UI' mandate can be respected without accidental duplication?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a lean, second loop-prep implementation guide scoped to the first vertical slice and then delivers architecture, contracts, sequence, and constraints for that slice.
apparent_themes: Registry as single source of truth
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a new, thin, addressable, motion-coherent front-end surface for the Company instrument without destabilizing the existing PWA, using the bridge API as the sole source of truth and calibrating depth via Tim's reaction to the first slice.
novelty: It inverts the usual front-end workflow by designing the resting empty state first, couples UI addressing with motion identity, and treats a new binding file as a new lens with zero front-end changes.
contribution: It contributes a scoped, buildable plan for the first Instrument Surface slice, including the projection contract, address spine convention, seed geometry render math, shared motion system, three form-factor layout modules, build sequence, and explicit guardrails against editing the legacy app or engine.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for the first vertical slice of the Instrument Surface, a fresh React/Vite app on port 5174 that renders an addressable, animated, seed-geometry instrument wheel from the /api/projection contract across desktop, portrait, and landscape layouts without modifying the existing canvas/app.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a lean, build-ordered plan that makes the first Instrument Surface slice real enough to render and get Tim's design reaction, grounded in the bridge API and mandate constraints.
